rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Linear atelectasis in the right middle lobe, lingular segments, and basal segments bilaterally. Pulmonary nodules are present; on series 5, as follows:
In the right lung:
- In the upper lobe:
  - Subpleural solid nodule measuring up to 3 mm (image 112).
  - Two subsolid nodules measuring up to 4 mm and 3 mm, respectively (image 65).
Also in the upper lobe, a calcification measuring up to 3 mm is present (image 67).
In the left lung:
- In the lower lobe:
  - Perifissural nodule measuring up to 5 mm (image 114).
No pleural effusion.
The trachea and central bronchi are patent.
Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy.
Heart: No cardiomegaly. Mild coronary calcification (right coronary artery). The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Pericardial fluid measures up to 1.1 cm in thickness.
Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated; wall calcification is present. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.
Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with forming osteophytes and Schmorl's nodes (at the L2, T12, and L1 levels). Decreased bone density in the vertebrae at the T11-T12 level to +118 HU, at the L1 level to less than +95 HU. Healing fractures of the ribs (anterior portions of the 7th and 8th ribs on the right, and the 10th rib on the left).
Upper abdomen: In the left kidney parenchyma (upper third), a lesion with well-defined smooth margins and fat density measures up to 9 × 6 mm.
Thyroid gland: In the left lobe, a hypodense lesion measures up to 6 mm in diameter, with well-defined smooth margins.
rad_impression_en:
1. CT appearance of a small number of pulmonary nodules of different density: small subsolid nodules in the right lung, small solid nodules in both lungs; per Fleischner Society recommendations (2017), no further CT follow-up is required.

2. Mild coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Moderate pericardial effusion.

3. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

4. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with forming osteophytes and Schmorl's nodes. Decreased bone density at the T11-L1 levels. Healing rib fractures bilaterally.

5. Fat-density hypodense lesion in the left kidney may correspond to angiomyolipoma; per Management of the Incidental Renal Mass on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee (2018), CT follow-up is not required.

6. Thyroid nodules; per Managing Incidental Thyroid Nodules Detected on Imaging: White Paper of the ACR Incidental Thyroid Findings Committee (2014), no further evaluation is required.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Pulmonary nodules are present; on series 5, as follows:
In the right upper lobe:
- Two ground-glass nodules measure up to 4 mm and 3 mm (image 65).
- Calcified nodule measures up to 3 mm (image 67), healed granuloma, requires no follow-up.
In the right middle lobe:
- Subpleural solid nodule measures up to 3 mm (image 112).
In the left lower lobe:
- Perifissural nodule measures up to 5 mm (image 114), perifissural lymph node, requires no follow-up.
Linear atelectasis in the right lower lobe. No pleural effusion. The trachea and central bronchi are patent. Wall thickening of the subsegmental and segmental bronchi with secretions.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. Hyperdense areas at the level of the right coronary artery. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Pericardial fluid measures up to 1.1 cm in thickness.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated; moderate wall calcification is present. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with forming osteophytes and Schmorl's nodes (at the L2, T12, and L1 levels). Decreased bone density in the vertebrae at the T11-T12 level to +118 HU, at the L1 level to less than +95 HU (CT signs of demineralization). Healed fractures of the ribs (the 7th and 8th ribs on the right, and the 10th rib on the left).

Upper abdomen: In the left kidney parenchyma (upper third), a lesion with well-defined smooth margins and fat density measures up to 9 × 6 mm.

Thyroid gland: In the left lobe, a hypodense lesion measures up to 6 mm in diameter.
val_impression_en:
1. In the right upper lobe, two ground-glass nodules. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following: Follow-up chest CT at 3-6 months to confirm persistence. If stable, consider chest CT at 2 and 4 years. In the right middle lobe, subpleural solid nodule measures up to 3 mm; in case of high risk, optional CT follow-up after 12 months. Wall thickening of the subsegmental and segmental bronchi with secretions may represent bronchitis.

2. Hyperdense areas at the level of the right coronary artery may be calcifications. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

3. Moderate pericardial effusion.

4. Fat-density hypodense lesion in the left kidney may correspond to angiomyolipoma; per Management of the Incidental Renal Mass on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee (2018), CT follow-up is not required.

5. Thyroid nodules; per Managing Incidental Thyroid Nodules Detected on Imaging: White Paper of the ACR Incidental Thyroid Findings Committee (2014), no further evaluation is required.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Pulmonary nodules in series 5 are as follows:
Right upper lobe:
- Solid subpleural nodule measuring 6 mm on image 51 (no change).
Right lower lobe:
- Solid subpleural nodule measuring 5 mm on image 191 (not previously visualized).
Left lower lobe:
- Solid subpleural nodule measuring 5 mm on image 199 (no change).
Confluent centrilobular emphysema in both lungs. Bronchial walls are irregularly thickened bilaterally, with bronchial contents in the lumen of some bronchi (predominantly at the level of the right middle and lower lobes). Mild discoid atelectasis in both lungs. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy is identified.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification is present in the left main, left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as severe.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, with severe wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is dilated to 29 cm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Severe degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Demineralization of bone.

Upper abdomen: In the right kidney, a lesion with density 1 HU measures 1.2 × 1.7 cm (assessment of size change is not possible because it is not fully included in the scan range). In the right kidney, a nonobstructing calculus measuring 4 mm (previously 2 mm). In the left kidney, a lesion with density 4 HU measures approximately 1.2 × 1.0 cm (previously approximately 7 × 8 mm). In the left kidney, a nonobstructing calculus measuring 3 mm (previously less than 2 mm). Dilatation of the left renal collecting system is noted.
rad_impression_en:
1. Solid nodule in the right lower lobe (not visualized on the study dated May 23, 2022); per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, follow-up CT in 12 months is recommended. Solid nodules in the right upper lobe and left lower lobe are stable compared with CT from May 23, 2022; per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, additional CT follow-up is not recommended. Confluent centrilobular emphysema. Bronchial contents and bronchial wall thickening in both lungs, which may correspond to bronchitis.

2. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as severe. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

3. Dilated main pulmonary artery (2.9 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

4. Severe atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

5. Bilateral renal lesions; per Management of the Incidental Renal Mass on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee, 2018, follow-up studies are not required.

6. Left hydronephrosis.

7. Bilateral nonobstructing renal calculi.

8. Demineralization of bone.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: In the right upper lobe, solid subpleural nodule measuring 6 mm on image 51, series 5 (no change). There are a few micronodules in both lungs up to 1–2 mm (no change). Severe confluent centrilobular emphysema in both lungs. Bronchial walls are thickened, bronchial contents in the lumen of some bronchi (predominantly at the level of the right middle and lower lobes). Mild discoid atelectasis in both lungs. In the right lower lobe, there is a paravertebral area of osteophyte-induced pulmonary fibrosis. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy is identified.

Heart: No cardiomegaly, mild enlargement of the left ventricle. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification is present in the left main, left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as severe.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, with severe wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is dilated to 29 cm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Severe degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. In the ribs, multiple low-density lesions, probably fat (for example, images 117, 119, 124), may correspond to extramedullary hematopoiesis, unchanged compared with chest CT on May 23, 2022 performed with a referral diagnosis of polycythemia.

Upper abdomen: In the right kidney, a lesion with density 1 HU measures 1.2 × 1.7 cm (assessment of size change is not possible because it is not fully included in the scan range). In the right kidney, a nonobstructing calculus measuring 4 mm (previously 2 mm). In the left kidney, a lesion with density 4 HU measures approximately 1.2 × 1.0 cm (previously approximately 7 × 8 mm). In the left kidney, a nonobstructing calculus measuring 3 mm (previously less than 2 mm). Bilateral hydronephrosis.
val_impression_en:
1. Solid nodule in the right upper lobe and a few micronodules in both lungs are stable compared with CT from May 23, 2022; per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, additional CT follow-up is not recommended. According to ACR Lung-RADS guidelines guidelines, Category 1 (Negative), definitely benign nodules.

2. Severe centrilobular emphysema. Pulmonary emphysema on CT is an independent risk factor for lung cancer. Consider enrollment in a low-dose CT (LDCT) lung cancer screening program if the patient meets eligibility criteria; today's CT may serve as a baseline for future screening (according to ACR Lung-RADS guidelines for Category 1, annual screening with low-dose chest CT in 12 months is recommended).

3. Bronchial contents and bronchial wall thickening in both lungs, which may correspond to bronchitis.

4. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as severe. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

5. Dilated main pulmonary artery (2.9 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

6. Severe atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

7. Bilateral renal lesions; per Management of the Incidental Renal Mass on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee, 2018, follow-up is not required. Bilateral hydronephrosis. Bilateral nonobstructing renal calculi.

Disclaimer: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura:
Linear atelectasis in the right middle lobe, lingular, and basal segments bilaterally.
Previously noted ground-glass nodules in the right lung have completely resolved.
A few perifissural nodule in the left lung measuring up to 3 mm (image 98), unchanged (likely a perifissural lymph node).
No pleural effusion.
The trachea and main bronchi are patent.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila:
No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart:
Cardiac chambers are enlarged (cardiothoracic ratio greater than 1/2).
No significant pericardial effusion.
Aortic valve calcification.
Moderate coronary calcification (more pronounced in the left anterior descending artery and in the proximal right coronary artery). The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

Great vessels:
Thoracic aorta is dilated up to 4.4 cm, with wall calcification.
Main pulmonary artery is dilated up to 4.0 cm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall:
Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation.
Decreased density of vertebral bodies at the T11-L1 level to +123 HU.
Decreased height of the T7 vertebral body up to 25.5%.
Partially included in the scan range: C7-C6 transpedicular vertebral fixation.
Osteosclerotic lesion of the posterior segment of the left 2nd rib measuring up to 8 mm.

Upper abdomen:
Gallbladder stones measuring up to 5 mm, without gallbladder wall thickening.
rad_impression_en:
1. Previously noted ground-glass nodules in the right lung have completely resolved. No pulmonary nodules requiring further follow-up per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations are identified.

2. Dilated main pulmonary artery (4.0 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

3. Moderate coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

4. Cardiomegaly.

5. Aortic valve calcification.

6. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta with dilation of the ascending aorta.

7. Decreased vertebral body density (CT signs of demineralization) at the T11-L1 level, decreased height of the T7 vertebral body, C7-C6 transpedicular fixation (partially included in the scan range in the lower cervical spine), thoracic spondylosis. Osteosclerotic lesion of the left 2nd rib (Bone-RADS 1).

8. Gallbladder stones.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Multiple ground-glass nodules in the right lung noted on December 22, 2025, have completely resolved. A few perifissural nodules in the left lung measure up to 3 mm (image 98), unchanged (likely a perifissural lymph node), require no follow-up. Multiple small pleural calcifications on both sides in the lower parts, unchanged. Linear atelectasis in the right middle lobe, lingular, and basal segments bilaterally. Gravitational changes. No pleural effusion. The trachea and main bronchi are patent.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: The heart is enlarged due to increased size of the chambers. Multiple fatty inclusions of the myocardium of the ventricles may correspond to the consequence of ischemic heart disease. Pericardial effusion with a thickness of 6 mm (previously in a smaller volume). Mild aortic and mitral valve calcifications. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left main, left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is dilated up to 4.4 cm, with moderate wall calcification, unchanged. Main pulmonary artery is dilated up to 3.7 cm, unchanged.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased density of vertebral bodies at the T11-L1 level to +123 HU, decreased height of the T7 vertebral body up to 25.5% (CT signs of demineralization). Partially included in the scan range: C7-C6 transpedicular vertebral fixation. Osteosclerotic lesion of the posterior segment of the left 2nd rib measuring up to 8 mm, unchanged compared to Chest CT on December 22, 2025.

Upper abdomen: Gallbladder stones measuring up to 5 mm, without gallbladder wall thickening. Multiple small hypodense liver lesions up to 5 mm, unchanged. ACR White Paper recommendations (Gore, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2017;14:1429-1437) suggest the following: For patients with low risk of having a malignant hepatic lesion, no need for further follow-up. For patients with high risk of having a malignant hepatic lesion, follow-up MRI in 3 to 6 months is recommended for further evaluation.
val_impression_en:
1. Multiple ground-glass nodules in the right lung noted on December 22, 2025, have completely resolved, were likely inflammatory/infectious changes. No pulmonary nodules requiring further follow-up per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations are identified.

2. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left main, left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

3. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.7 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

4. Enlargement of the heart chambers. Small-volume pericardial effusion. Mild aortic and mitral valve calcifications. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta with dilation of the ascending aorta up to 4.4 cm.

5. Cholelithiasis.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura:
Pulmonary nodules in series 5 are as follows:
Right upper lobe:
- Subsolid nodule measuring 7 mm (previously 9 mm) on image 95.
- Micronodules up to 2 mm, unchanged.

Multiple multiloculated air-filled cysts persist in the lungs, unchanged, located in the right upper and middle lobes and in both lower lobes. Locally dilated lumina of segmental and subsegmental bronchi also persist in the lower lobes, with cystic bronchiectasis.
In the supradiaphragmatic right lower lobe, there is a linear band with a dilated subsegmental bronchial lumen within it.
No pleural effusion.
Mild thickening of the bronchial walls in the lower lobes. Small amount of mural pathologic contents in the tracheal lumen.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila:
No mass or lymphadenopathy. Bifurcation lymph nodes are partially calcified.

Heart:
No cardiomegaly. Small pericardial effusion with separation of the pericardial layers up to 5 mm. Coronary calcification is present in the left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as mild.

Great vessels:
Thoracic aorta is not dilated. Main pulmonary artery is dilated to 3.9 cm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall:
Severe degenerative changes of the spine (osteochondrosis, spondylosis). Osteosclerotic lesion in the L1 vertebral body with well-defined margins, measuring 1.3 × 1.0 cm, density up to 550 HU (unchanged).

Upper abdomen:
Cystic lesion of the right renal sinus measuring 4.5 × 4.0 cm. Decreased density of the liver parenchyma to 48 HU.
Right adrenal lesion with density of -22 HU, measuring 1.5 × 1.4 cm; left adrenal lesion measuring 1.6 × 1.4 cm, density -8 HU. Cystic lesion in the right hepatic lobe measuring 8 × 5 mm, unchanged.
rad_impression_en:
1. Subsolid nodule in the right upper lobe with partial decrease in size. Decrease in size from 9 mm to 7 mm is a favorable sign; however, given its persistence and size of 6 mm or greater in a high-risk patient, continued follow-up is recommended, with follow-up CT in 12 months per the 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations. Stable micronodules 2 mm or smaller do not require dedicated follow-up.

2. Multifocal cystic involvement of the lungs, unchanged.

3. Cystic bronchiectasis in lung bases, unchanged.

4. Mild thickening of the bronchial walls in the lower lobes and mural contents in the tracheal lumen, findings of bronchitis.

5. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as mild.
The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for a comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

6. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.9 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

7. Low-density adrenal lesions measuring up to 1.6 cm, likely benign. No additional imaging is required.

8. Cystic lesion in the region of the right renal sinus. Further evaluation is recommended with CT with intravenous contrast.

9. Unchanged cystic lesion of the right hepatic lobe, most likely a simple cyst.

10. Osteosclerotic lesion in the L1 vertebral body. In the absence of oncologic history and given stable size, follow-up with CT or MRI in 12 months may be considered to confirm stability and exclude growth.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Pulmonary nodules in series 5 are as follows:
Right upper lobe:
- Part-solid nodule measures 7 mm (image 95), on Chest CT on May 19, 2025 9 mm, on Chest CT on December 31, 2024 1.1 cm × 0.9 cm.
Right lower lobe:
- Calcified nodule measures 4 mm (image 200), unchanged, healed granuloma, requires no follow-up.
Micronodules up to 2 mm in both lungs, unchanged.
Multiple multiloculated cysts persist in the lungs, unchanged compared to Chest CT on December 31, 2024. Cysts are located peribronchially in the right upper and middle lobes and in both lower lobes, reticulations are noted along the periphery of the cysts. In the upper lobes there is mild centrilobular emphysema, a few areas of paraseptal emphysema (image 84, on the left). A few linear atelectasis in the basal lungs. No pleural effusion. Mild thickening of the bronchial walls in the lower lobes. Small amount of mucus in the tracheal lumen.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass. The mediastinal lymph nodes are numerous, up to 1.0 cm in size; compared to CT on May 19, 2025, an increase in the size of the lymph nodes by 1-2 mm along the short axis is noted. Subcarinal lymph nodes are partially calcified (healed granulomatous disease).

Heart: No cardiomegaly. Pericardial effusion with thickness up to 1.2 cm, unchanged. Coronary calcification is present in the left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated, with moderate wall calcification. Main pulmonary artery is dilated to 4.3 cm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the spine. Osteosclerotic lesion in the L1 vertebral body with well-defined margins, measuring 1.3 × 1.0 cm, 550 HU (unchanged).

Upper abdomen: Cystic lesion of the right renal sinus measuring 4.5 × 4.0 cm. Cystic lesion in the right hepatic lobe measuring 8 × 5 mm, unchanged.
Right adrenal lesion with density of -22 HU, measuring 1.5 × 1.4 cm; diffuse thickening of the left adrenal gland; unchanged.
val_impression_en:
1. Part-solid nodule in the right upper lobe with decrease in size compared to Chest CT on May 19, 2025, December 31, 2024, probably corresponds to an inflammatory process. Stable 2 mm micronodules do not require follow-up.

2. Diffuse cystic lung disease, unchanged compared to December 31, 2024.

3. Mild centrilobular and paraseptal emphysema in upper lobes. Pulmonary emphysema on CT is an independent risk factor for lung cancer. Consider enrollment in a low-dose CT (LDCT) lung cancer screening program if the patient meets eligibility criteria; today's CT may serve as a baseline for future screening. Note: Recommendation applies unless the patient has a history of lung cancer, known pulmonary nodules or lung mass, receives routine chest CTs/screening, or is receiving palliative care.

4. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for a comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

5. Dilated main pulmonary artery represents pulmonary hypertension, which is consistent with echocardiogram on February 20, 2026.

6. Trace pericardial effusion.

7. Cystic lesion of the right renal sinus. ACR White Paper recommendations (Herts, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2018;15:264-273) suggest the following: Likely benign cyst. No need for further work-up.

8. Unchanged cystic lesion of the right hepatic lobe. ACR White Paper recommendations (Gore, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2017;14:1429-1437) suggest the following: For patients with low risk of having a malignant hepatic lesion, no need for further follow-up.

9. Right adrenal lesion and diffuse thickening of the left adrenal gland, unchanged. ACR White Paper recommendations (Mayo-Smith, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2017;14:1038-1044) suggest the following: Benign lesion. No need for further imaging follow-up.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura:
Solid nodules identified in the lungs are as follows on series 5:
In the right lower lobe:
- Nodule measuring up to 1.0 × 0.7 cm (image 166) - previously 7 mm.
In the left upper lobe:
- Nodule measuring up to 4 mm (image 166) - unchanged.
A small number of calcifications are also present in the lungs, largest in the left lower lobe measuring up to 7 mm.
Linear atelectatic changes in the middle lobe, right paravertebral.
Reticular changes in the subpleural regions of the lungs, with areas of thickened interlobular interstitium in the lower basal regions on a background of gravitational changes.
No pleural effusion.
Trachea and major bronchi are patent.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila:
No mass or lymphadenopathy.
Calcifications in 10L lymph nodes.

Heart:
No cardiomegaly.
No significant pericardial effusion.
Severe coronary artery calcification (extensive calcified atherosclerotic plaques in the right coronary artery and left circumflex artery, less pronounced in the left anterior descending artery).
Aortic valve calcification.

Great vessels:
Thoracic aorta is not dilated, with wall calcification.
Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall:
Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with formation of large osteophytes.
Decreased density of vertebral bodies at T11-L1 to +124 HU.

Upper abdomen:
Low-density (-11 HU) masses in the adrenal glands: in the lateral limb on the right measuring up to 2.6 × 1.5 cm, in the body of the left adrenal gland measuring 2.2 × 1.2 cm (unchanged from the prior study and compared with CT dated February 03, 2025).
In the parenchyma of the mid left kidney, a fluid-density lesion measuring up to 1.0 × 0.9 cm with sharp margins (unchanged compared with CT dated February 03, 2025).
rad_impression_en:
1. Solid nodule in the right lower lobe - given increase in size compared with the prior study, per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, consider PET/CT or biopsy. Small solid nodule in the left upper lobe, unchanged. Interstitial lung changes (nonspecific interstitial pneumonia?) visually unchanged. Pulmonary calcifications.

2. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as severe.
The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for a comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.
Note: Recommendation applies unless the patient has known CAD, prior CABG/stent, trauma indications or intraoperative CTs.

3. Aortic valve calcification. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

4. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased vertebral body density at T11-L1.

5. Adrenal masses of benign phenotype: according to "Management of Incidental Adrenal Masses: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee, 2017", no additional evaluation or follow-up imaging is required.

6. Hypodense lesion in the parenchyma of the left kidney of fluid/cystic density: according to "Management of the Incidental Renal Mass on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee, 2018", in the absence of diseases and/or genetic syndromes predisposing to renal neoplasms, as well as in the absence of primary malignancies with a high likelihood of metastasizing to the kidneys (lung cancer, lymphoma, or melanoma), no further follow-up/additional evaluation is required.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Solid nodules identified in the lungs are as follows on series 5:
In the right lower lobe:
- Nodule measures up to 1.0 × 0.8 cm (image 166), On Chest CT on January 19, 2026, nodule measures 8 × 6 mm. On Chest CT on February 3, 2025, the nodule is not identified. 
In the left lower lobe:
- Calcified nodules measure 7 mm and 3 mm (image 223), unchanged.
Multiple, predominantly subpleural and basal, areas of ground-glass opacity with reticulations, thickening of the interlobular interstitium, traction bronchiectasis, and interval progression compared with Chest CT on February 3, 2025 due to the appearance of new areas of ground-glass. Mosaic attenuation is present in the lower lobes. No honeycombing is identified. In the right lower lobe, there is a paravertebral area of osteophyte-induced fibrosis. No pleural effusion. Trachea and major bronchi are patent.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy. Calcifications in the left pulmonary lymph nodes.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. Severe coronary artery calcification, calcified atherosclerotic plaques in the right coronary artery, left circumflex artery, left anterior descending artery. 

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated, with moderate wall calcification. Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with formation of large osteophytes. Degenerative changes of shoulder joints. Decreased density of vertebral bodies at T11-L1 to +124 HU (CT signs of demineralization).

Upper abdomen: Low-density (-11 HU) lesions in the adrenal glands (benign adenomas): in the lateral limb on the right measuring 1.5 cm, in the body of the left adrenal gland measuring 1.2 cm (unchanged).
In the parenchyma of the left kidney, a fluid-density lesion measuring up to 1.0 × 0.9 cm with sharp margins (unchanged), likely benign cyst.
val_impression_en:
1. Solid nodule in the right lower lobe, increased in size compared with Chest CT on January 19, 2026, likely of neoplastic etiology; consider PET/CT or biopsy.

2. Interstitial lung disease with a slight interval worsening compared with Chest CT on February 3, 2025 may correspond to fibrotic non-specific interstitial pneumonia or probable usual interstitial pneumonia according to Diagnostic HRCT criteria for usual interstitial pneumonia pattern - ATS/ERS/JRS/ALAT (2018).

3. Calcified nodules in the left lower lobe and calcifications in the left pulmonary lymph nodes may represent healed granulomatous disease, require no follow-up.

4. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as severe. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for a comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Note: Recommendation applies unless the patient has known CAD, prior CABG/stent, trauma indications or intraoperative CTs.

5. Bilateral adrenal benign adenomas, unchanged. ACR White Paper recommendations (Mayo-Smith, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2017;14:1038-1044) suggest the following: No need for further imaging follow-up. Consider biochemical evaluation to determine functional status.

6. Likely benign cyst of the left kidney. ACR White Paper recommendations (Herts, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2018;15:264-273) suggest the following: No need for further work-up.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
